mnemonics: Virtual Functions Cascade Down the Family Tree
Heir-archical: The Right Heir Gets the Inheritance
V-Table: Virtual calls navigate the hierarchy like a family tree
keyPoints: A virtual function in a base class can be overridden at any level of the inheritance hierarchy, not just by immediate derived classes.
The function called is determined by the actual object type at runtime, allowing for polymorphic behavior through deep inheritance chains.
Once a function is declared virtual, it remains virtual in all subsequent derived classes, even without explicitly using the 'virtual' keyword.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare the base class function as 'virtual', which prevents runtime polymorphism and causes base class function calls even with derived objects.
Assuming that overriding a virtual function in a middle-level class prevents further overriding in deeper derived classes (it does not).
Incorrectly using different function signatures (return types or parameters) in derived classes, which does not constitute a valid override and creates a new function instead.
